CHANGE RECORD 
 
When the quality of the products change s, the QuID is updated an d a row is added to this table.  The 
third column specifies which sections  or sub-sections have been updated.  The fourth column should 
mention the version of the product to which the change applies. Column 5 should include the names of 
the author(s) doing the changes. Including the name in c olumn 6 shows that the document has been 
validated by the TAC/MFC PQ leader or deputy (or TAC/MFC PQ leader or the deputy). This validation 
means the document was checked for contents, formats and consistency with other documents.

KEY DEFINITIONS 
Glacier ice Ice that has formed by the accumulation and compression of snow over 
time. 
Marginal ice zone The region of an ice cover which is affected by waves and swell 
penetrating into the ice from the open ocean". It is a transitional zone 
between open water and dense pack ice. 
Multi-year ice Sea ice that has survived at least one summer melt season 
Pack ice Sea ice that is not attached to the shoreline and drifts freely on the ocean, 
driven by winds and currents. 
Sea Ice Concentration  Fraction (percentage) of the sea surface area of the grid cell  containing 
sea-ice. 
Sea Ice floe size An ice floe is a relatively flat piece of floating ice that is at least 20 meters 
in width.  Sea ice floes are classified by size, from the smallest to the 
largest: ice cakes (less than 20 meters), small floes (20–100 m)
